plang.note: all 6 fails are deliberately-failing fixture inputs (_fixtures_fail/*, _fixtures_sensitive/*.fixture.goal); not re-run for v7 (no PLang surface change), values carried from coder baseline

coverage.note: v7 changed lines (_active instance scope, copy-on-write Enter, parent-restoring Releaser) are not exercised by any existing test; reverting either fix would not turn the suite red.

findings.issue: B1 fix (`_active` static→instance) has no regression test. Walked all Stage8 tests — none would fail if `static` were reintroduced. Compounded by `Binding.Id = Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N")[..8]` (Binding/this.cs:89) so cross-channel id collisions cannot occur through the public API.
findings.impact: Future refactors (deterministic binding ids, another consumer of `_active`) could re-introduce `static` and ship green.
findings.suggestion: Direct unit test on `Channel.Events.@this`: instantiate two `Events.@this`, call `evA.Enter("X")`, assert `evB.IsActive("X") == false`. ~3 lines, fails immediately if `_active` becomes static.

findings.issue: L1 fix (copy-on-write `Enter` + parent-restoring `Releaser`) has no regression test. Coder explicitly acknowledged 'no current callsite' exercises parallel fan-out from a binding handler. Stage8's `BeforeWriteHandler_WritesToSameChannel_NoInfiniteLoop` only does sequential await.
findings.impact: A future binding handler that uses `Task.WhenAll` for parallel logging/fan-out would race on the shared HashSet if L1 were reverted, surfacing as a flaky `InvalidOperationException` in production rather than at test time.
findings.suggestion: Test: `using (events.Enter("A")) { await Task.WhenAll(Task.Run(() => { using (events.Enter("B")){} }), Task.Run(() => { using (events.Enter("C")){} })); assert events.IsActive("A") && !events.IsActive("B") && !events.IsActive("C"); }`. With L1 the parent's set is unaffected by children; without L1 it leaks B/C or the HashSet throws under contention.

summary: Suite is green (2760/2760 C#). v7 change is conceptually correct. Both fixes are defensive/structural and not regressed — but they are also untested. Two minor missing-coverage findings; non-blocking. Recommend a small follow-up commit (≤10 lines of test) to lock in the fixes before consumers arrive. Then security.
